coin_profile.name: Ondo
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Ondo (ONDO) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #45 by market cap.

ONDO.name: Ondo

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

chart_zones.mos_note: Margin of safety present — price near or below speculative fair value during accumulation (bear market) phase.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 4.3/10 and survivability 40.0%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -37.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.3/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-37.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 40.0% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: DeFiLlama has no fee data for this token. This typically means the protocol does not generate trackable on-chain fees, or its fee reporting is not yet indexed. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value (MC/Volume ratio) confirmed the NVT signal and contributed to the median pool. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Limited price history available (2 year(s)). This token may be recently listed or have insufficient on-chain data.

ONDO.iv_method: Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark

watchlist_signal.reason: Weak fundamentals or heavily overextended price relative to value — higher-quality opportunities available elsewhere.

Any positive news catalyst — exchange listing, partnership, or protocol upgrade — could trigger a sharp near-term rally from current levels

Up 34% in the past week — rapid short-term move increases likelihood of a pullback before the next leg higher
Without a near-term catalyst, Ondo may continue to consolidate or drift lower

bull_points: Multi-year on-chain fee data confirms the protocol has generated real revenue across both bull and bear market cycles
As the crypto ecosystem matures, Ondo's established position should compound in value
Continued development activity and ecosystem growth support a constructive long-term view
bear_points: FDV is 2.0x market cap — substantial token unlock pressure over the coming years could persistently weigh on price

metric_assessments.fee_growth: Fee Growth is a concern (0.55 fee/tvl %). This dimension is below par and worth monitoring closely.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: Capital Efficiency is middling. The numbers are acceptable but leave room for improvement.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: Holder Value Accrual is middling (15.0 % of fees to holders). The numbers are acceptable but leave room for improvement.
metric_assessments.network_demand: Network Demand is a clear strength (13.94 volume/MC %), placing this token well above most peers on this dimension.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: Dilution Control is a concern (48.69 circulating %). This dimension is below par and worth monitoring closely.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: Protocol Maturity is solid (45 MC rank), meeting the expectations you would expect for a protocol at this stage.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Ondo carries a Dangerous rating because its fundamentals score only 4.3/10 as a Cash-Flow Protocol, with notable weaknesses in fee growth. Significant future token dilution potential adds further pressure. Dangerous-rated tokens carry a higher risk of permanent capital loss and should only be held with strict position sizing and a clear exit plan.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Ondo is a Cash-Flow Protocol with weak fundamentals, scoring 4.3/10 overall. Its clearest strength is network demand, while fee growth remains the main area to watch. Key risks: future token dilution (FDV/MC ratio: 2.0x); low fee growth score (1/10); only 49% of supply currently circulating. Ondo warrants caution and should only be held as a small position with a clear thesis and defined risk limits.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Ondo scores 4.3/10 (Dangerous) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

coin_profile.description: Optimism is an Ethereum Layer 2 using optimistic rollup technology. It pioneered the Superchain vision — a network of OP Stack chains including Coinbase's Base. OP is the governance token. Like Arbitrum, sequencer revenue currently goes to the foundation rather than token holders.

coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 4.5/10 and survivability 53.3%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -29.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.5/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-29.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) did not contribute to the median: the implied fair value fell outside the 0.1x–10x price bounds (volume is very low or very high relative to circulating supply). Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 1 method contributed to the median — the valuation should be treated as a rough anchor, not a precise fair value. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

coin_profile.name: OpenGradient
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 4.7/10 and survivability 23.3%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -48.1% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.7/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-48.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 23.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value (MC/Volume ratio) confirmed the NVT signal and contributed to the median pool. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Historical price data unavailable for this token. Forecast chart shows projected values only.
